skill.name: dropbox-automation
skill.description: Automate Dropbox file management, sharing, search, uploads, downloads, and folder operations via Rube MCP (Composio).
skill.summary: Streamline Dropbox workflows with MCP-powered automation for file operations, sharing, and folder management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 220 external_commands patterns and 1 network pattern, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is documentation only - backticks denote MCP tool names (e.g., DROPBOX_SEARCH_FILE_OR_FOLDER), not shell execution. The network URL is the official Rube MCP endpoint. No malicious intent or security vulnerabilities found.

content.user_title: Automate Dropbox File Operations with AI
content.value_statement: Managing files across Dropbox manually consumes time and creates friction in workflows. This skill enables AI assistants to handle file uploads, downloads, searches, sharing, and folder operations automatically through the Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Search for files and folders by name, content, or file type with pagination support
Upload and download files, including batch operations and folder exports
Create and manage shared links with configurable access, expiration, and password protection
Create, move, rename, and delete files and folders including batch operations
List folder contents recursively with filtering and media metadata support
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server configured at https://rube.app/mcp
Dropbox OAuth connection must be established before operations
Some operations like URL saving and batch jobs run asynchronously with polling required
Search is limited to 10,000 total matches across all pagination pages

use_cases.title: Automated Document Backup
use_cases.description: Set up workflows to automatically upload project files to organized Dropbox folders, create timestamped backups, and maintain versioned archives for critical documents.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and project managers needing reliable file backup automation

use_cases.title: Team File Sharing Automation
use_cases.description: Automate the creation of password-protected shared links with expiration dates for client deliverables, ensuring secure and time-limited access to sensitive files.
use_cases.target_user: Freelancers and agencies sharing work with clients

use_cases.title: Content Organization and Migration
use_cases.description: Bulk move, rename, and reorganize files across Dropbox folders, migrate content between directory structures, and clean up duplicate or outdated files.
use_cases.target_user: Content managers and operations teams managing large file libraries

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Dropbox for all PDF files in the Documents folder containing 'budget' in the name or content. Show me the first page of results with file paths and sizes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding specific documents in a large Dropbox repository

prompt_templates.title: File Upload with Organization
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the attached quarterly report to /Reports/2026/Q1/ directory. If the folder does not exist, create it. Then create a shared link with team-only access and no expiration.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Folder Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: List all files in /Downloads folder that are older than 6 months. Move image files to /Archive/Images and document files to /Archive/Documents. Delete any temporary files with .tmp extension.
prompt_templates.scenario: Automated folder maintenance and cleanup operations

prompt_templates.title: Secure Client File Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest version of 'ProjectAlpha_Final.pdf' in /Projects/ClientWork/. Create a password-protected shared link that expires in 7 days, allows downloads, and is accessible only to team members. Return the link URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Secure time-limited file sharing with clients

output_examples.input: Search for budget spreadsheets in the Finance folder
output_examples.output: Found 5 matching files: budget_2026.xlsx (2.1 MB), Q1_budget.xlsx (1.4 MB), Q2_budget.xlsx (1.5 MB), annual_budget_draft.xlsx (3.2 MB), department_budgets.xlsx (945 KB). All located in /Finance/ folder. Use DROPBOX_SEARCH_CONTINUE if more results needed.

output_examples.input: Upload report.pdf to /Reports and create a shared link
output_examples.output: File uploaded successfully to /Reports/report.pdf. Shared link created: https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc123xyz/report.pdf?dl=0 (Team access, no expiration, downloads allowed).

output_examples.input: List contents of /Projects/Archive with file counts
output_examples.output: Folder /Projects/Archive/ contains 23 items: 15 files (8 PDFs, 4 images, 3 documents) and 8 subfolders. Total size: 156 MB. Most recent modification: 2026-01-15.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing any Dropbox operations
Validate file paths with DROPBOX_GET_METADATA before operations to avoid path_not_found errors
Check for existing shared links with DROPBOX_LIST_SHARED_LINKS before creating new ones to avoid duplicates
Use batch operations (DROPBOX_MOVE_BATCH, DROPBOX_DELETE_BATCH) when processing multiple files to reduce API calls
Handle pagination cursors properly - follow has_more flags to ensure complete results from search and folder listing
anti_patterns: Do not skip pagination - always follow cursors when has_more is true to avoid silently missing results
Avoid hardcoding paths without validation - API-returned path_display may differ in casing from user input
Do not create shared links without checking existing links first - causes 409 Conflict errors
Never assume synchronous completion for SAVE_URL or batch operations - poll job status or use wait parameter

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and do I need API keys?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server that provides access to various services including Dropbox. You add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client - no API keys needed initially. You will need to complete Dropbox OAuth through the Rube connection manager.

faq.question: How do I connect my Dropbox account?
faq.answer: Call DROPBOX_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'dropbox'. If no active connection exists, it returns an auth link. Follow that link to complete Dropbox OAuth, then confirm the connection status shows ACTIVE before running workflows.

faq.question: What is the maximum file size for uploads and downloads?
faq.answer: Dropbox supports files up to 350 GB. For folder downloads via DROPBOX_DOWNLOAD_ZIP, the total must be under 20 GB with no single file over 4 GB and fewer than 10,000 entries.

faq.question: How does pagination work for search results?
faq.answer: Search results return has_more: true with a cursor when additional results exist. Use DROPBOX_SEARCH_CONTINUE with the cursor to get the next page. Maximum 10,000 total matches across all pages.

faq.question: Can I automate recurring Dropbox tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. You can prompt the AI assistant to run scheduled workflows like 'Every Monday, upload the weekly report to /Reports/Weekly/ and share with the team.' The skill handles the file operations through MCP tool calls.

faq.question: What happens if a file upload conflicts with an existing file?
faq.answer: By default, uploads fail on conflict (mode: 'add'). Use mode: 'overwrite' to replace existing files, or set autorename: true to let Dropbox auto-rename the file (e.g., 'file (1).pdf').
